_comment: MANDATORY: after editing this file, run `python ops/apply_railway_service_config.py --all` BEFORE the next push. Service-instance fields (deploy.<service>.{startCommand, cronSchedule, restartPolicyType, source.repo, watchPatterns}) live on Railway's service-instance config and are only updated via the GraphQL serviceInstanceUpdate mutation. Without the apply, push triggers a rebuild but the new container still runs with the OLD startCommand/cron/etc — you get a build-vs-runtime config drift that's painful to diagnose (build log shows the new buildCommand; runtime crashes anyway because startCommand wasn't propagated). The only field Railway actually reads from this file at build time is `build.buildCommand`. See docs/OPERATIONS.md §1 'Build layout' and the project memory entry feedback_apply_after_railway_json.md.
_architecture: FOUR Railway services (event-driven shape per operator directive 2026-05-25 'event-driven like we had before' — replaces the consolidated 3-service cron shape from 2026-05-15): data-operations (the ONE scheduled seed-event cron; emits DATA_OPERATIONS_COMPLETE), engine-service (persistent — polls application_log for DATA_OPERATIONS_COMPLETE / DATA_REPAIR_COMPLETE, dispatches engine sweep, dispatches allocator on cadence=WEEKLY_FIRST_TRADING_DAY), lane-service (persistent — polls for ENGINE_DATA_REQUEST, runs deterministic data_repair), trade-monitor (persistent — Alpaca trade_updates websocket). Trade-monitor runs as its OWN service here (not co-hosted in engine-service as DA-3 does on Mac); engine-service therefore sets STE_DISABLE_TRADE_MONITOR_COTASK=1 to prevent duplicate Alpaca websocket connections. Mirrors the Mac launchd topology one-for-one (data-operations cron + engine-service persistent + lane-service persistent), with the trade-monitor co-task externalized for crash isolation. Hobby tier caps at 5 services; we use 4 to leave 1 slot for a dashboard / health endpoint.
_csv_archive_env_vars: R3 archive-substrate migration (PR feat/csv-archive-pluggable-backend-r3, 2026-05-21). Set these in Railway's service-variables UI (NOT here — secrets, and Railway reads service vars from its own store, not from railway.json). Unset → ephemeral local FS, which is the broken-on-Railway state R3 fixes. Required values when migrating: CSV_ARCHIVE_BACKEND=s3, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_ENDPOINT=<host:port>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_BUCKET=<bucket>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_KEY_ID=<access-key>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_SECRET=<secret-key>. Optional: CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_REGION (non-AWS S3-compatibles can leave blank), CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_SECURE='false' only for an in-cluster MinIO over plain HTTP (default 'true'). 2026-05-25: bucket provisioned in Cloudflare R2 (ste-archives, 91 archive files pre-seeded).
_engine_service_env_vars: STE_DISABLE_TRADE_MONITOR_COTASK=1 — REQUIRED for the engine-service service in this 4-service shape. Without it engine-service co-hosts its own TradeMonitor.run_forever() loop (Mac DA-3 default), which would race the separate trade-monitor Railway service for the same Alpaca trade_updates websocket. Alpaca rejects the second connection or thrashes between them. Set this only on the engine-service Railway service variables, NOT on trade-monitor or anywhere else.

build._build_note: Build into an explicit venv at /app/.deps (NOT /app/.venv — .gitignore line 8 strips that path from the runtime copy). Packages live inside /app, which Railpack copies wholesale into the runtime image; the venv's python binary symlinks back to the mise-managed interpreter at /mise/installs/python/<ver>/bin/python, which IS also copied. Why this over installing into mise's site-packages directly: Railpack only preserves the /mise/installs symlink, not its real target under /root/.local/share/mise/... so `/mise/shims/python -m pip install` succeeds at build time but the packages vanish on stage handoff. The venv-in-/app pattern sidesteps that entirely.

data-operations._comment: Scheduled cron — the ONE seed time-trigger in the event-driven topology. Runs the 6-stage data-operations workflow (DOWNLOAD/UPLOAD → VERIFY auditheal → VERIFY data_validation → SELF-HEAL → MATVIEW → DEEP AUDIT → COMPRESS → EMIT EVENT) and emits DATA_OPERATIONS_COMPLETE to platform.application_log on success. Cron: 21:30 UTC weekdays — 90 min after NYSE close (winter EST) and 30 min after (summer EDT), so daily bars are settled. ops.py --update refuses during regular session if invoked manually; the cron timing avoids that by construction. The engine sweep is NOT bundled here — engine-service is a separate persistent daemon that polls application_log for DATA_OPERATIONS_COMPLETE and fires the sweep itself.

engine-service._comment: Persistent event-driven daemon — polls platform.application_log on a 60s cursor for DATA_OPERATIONS_COMPLETE / DATA_REPAIR_COMPLETE events, dispatches the engine sweep (run_all_engines.sh) when found AND tpcore.calendar.is_trading_day() is True, also dispatches the allocator engine via the WEEKLY_FIRST_TRADING_DAY cadence in tpcore.engine_profile (NOT a separate cron). Also runs the day-rollover weekly-digest emit subprocess trigger. STE_DISABLE_TRADE_MONITOR_COTASK=1 disables the in-process trade-monitor co-task that the Mac DA-3 shape uses (since trade-monitor is its OWN Railway service in this 4-service topology). NEVER bundles the data refresh — that's data-operations' job. NEVER fires the engine sweep on a calendar-NO day. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-engine-sdlc-design.md + tpcore.engine_profile.

lane-service._comment: Persistent event-driven daemon — polls platform.application_log for ENGINE_DATA_REQUEST events, runs the deterministic data-repair co-task, emits DATA_REPAIR_COMPLETE (XOR DATA_REPAIR_ESCALATED) when a request is fully resolved. Single co-task in the deployed shape (no LLM triage in repo as of 2026-05-22; LLM lab/finder/monitor removed 2026-05-25). The deterministic cascade catalog is the COMPLETE self-heal layer. Sentinel: tests/test_lane_service_no_anthropic.py — reds the build if anthropic transitively leaks into the deployed import graph.

trade-monitor._comment: Persistent service — consumes Alpaca trade_updates and writes platform.open_orders + platform.aar_events. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-trade-monitor-design.md. Requires ipv6EgressEnabled=true (Supabase direct connection) and ALPACA_KEY/ALPACA_SECRET/ALPACA_PAPER in service variables. NOTE: must run as `python -m tpcore.trade_monitor` (not `python tpcore/trade_monitor.py`) — invoking the file directly puts tpcore/ on sys.path, which makes stdlib `import logging` resolve to the project's tpcore.logging package and crash with a circular-import error during interpreter bootstrap. In this 4-service shape, the trade-monitor co-task inside engine-service is DISABLED via STE_DISABLE_TRADE_MONITOR_COTASK=1 to prevent duplicate Alpaca websocket connections.
